attempted_job.description: Which of the following has the highest predictive validity in personnel selection in industry
options: An interview by the personnel manager

attempted_job.description: In client-centered case consultation, the consultant's primary goal is to:
options: work independently to provide the optimal intervention to a client.
help the consultee develop a stronger professional identity and sense of self.
help the consultee identify methods for evaluating his/her own professional services.
help the consultee to improve his/her communication skills with clients.
help the consultee determine how to work more effectively with a particular client.
help the consultee learn to manage his/her own professional stress and burnout.
help the consultee establish a structured plan for future client interactions.
work collaboratively with the consultee in delivering the optimal intervention to a client.
help a consultee enhance his/her skills and knowledge so that the consultee functions more effectively in the future.
help the consultee identify areas of personal growth and development.
attempted_job.correct_answer: help the consultee determine how to work more effectively with a particular client.

attempted_job.description: Nancy has the choice to spend one hour studying for an exam, mowing the lawn for one hour at a wage of $6, or babysitting her niece for one hour at a wage of $8. If we know that Nancy has chosen to study for the exam, which of the following is true?
options: The opportunity cost of studying is $6, which is equal to the benefit received from studying.
The opportunity cost of studying is $14, which is less than the benefit received from studying.
Nancy has no other options available besides studying, mowing the lawn, or babysitting.
Nancy values studying more than earning $6 from mowing the lawn.
Nancy is indifferent to both studying and mowing the lawn.
Nancy's behavior is irrational, since babysitting was clearly superior to all other options.
The benefit received from studying is less than the opportunity cost of $8.
The benefit received from studying is greater than the opportunity cost of $8.
The opportunity cost of studying is $8, but the benefit of studying is unmeasurable.
Nancy prefers babysitting to studying but chose to study instead.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The benefit received from studying is greater than the opportunity cost of $8.

attempted_job.description: The reaction of Br2(g) with Cl2(g) to form BrCl(g) has an equilibrium constant of 15.0 at a certain temperature. If 10.0 grams of BrCl is initially present in a 15.0-liter reaction vessel, what will the concentration of BrCl be at equilibrium?

The police interrogated a suspect regarding a series of burglaries. The suspect had attention deficit disorder, dyslexia and an IQ of 72. Prior to beginning they read a list of Miranda rights to him on a form that the suspect had in front of him. After the reading, he initialed each paragraph indicating he understood. At the bottom of the form was an express written waiver of the Miranda rights, which he signed. He had been interrogated in the past and was familiar with the procedure. During the four hours of interrogation he was given bathroom breaks, coffee, and a sandwich. At the end of the process, he made a logical and seemingly cogent confession to all of the burglaries in a taped statement. The defendant later moved to suppress the confession, arguing that he was coming down from drugs at the time and was intellectually unable to understand and comprehend the nature of the warnings or the waiver. What is the most likely decision of the court under the totality of the circumstances?
options: The suspect's past familiarity with the procedure proves he understood the situation and his confession is valid.
The suspect's signature on the waiver of Miranda rights is not enough to prove that he fully understood the situation.
The signing of a written waiver of Miranda rights is always proof that the suspect gave a voluntary confession.
The outward signs all indicate that the suspect understood the procedure and gave a voluntary confession despite his disabilities.
There are too many questionable deficiencies in the suspect's ability to understand what was going on and the confession must be suppressed.
The suspect was provided with basic needs during the interrogation, proving that it was not coercive.
The confession must be suppressed due to the suspect's intellectual disabilities and possible influence of drugs.
He was too confused to understand what was going on and the confession was coerced by the persistence of the police.
The suspect's disabilities make it impossible for him to understand the situation, thus the confession must be suppressed.
The suspect's comprehension of the Miranda rights is unclear, thus the confession must be reevaluated.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The outward signs all indicate that the suspect understood the procedure and gave a voluntary confession despite his disabilities.

attempted_job.description: One example of bad constitutive moral luck would be:
options: a person who donates to charity regularly.
a person who lived in Nazi Germany in 1940.
a person who drives drunk and hits a child.
a person who is naturally kind and generous.
a person who lived in the United States in the 1960s.
a person who drives carefully and avoids accidents.
a person who is by nature envious and ungenerous.
a person who lives in a peaceful, affluent country.
attempted_job.correct_answer: a person who is by nature envious and ungenerous.

attempted_job.description: An Earth satellite has a perigee of $300 \mathrm{~km}$ and an apogee of $3,500 \mathrm{~km}$ above Earth's surface. How far is the satellite above Earth when it has rotated $90^{\circ}$ around Earth from perigee?

Records reveal the following information: Quantity Cost Per Unit Inventory, Dec. 2 6 $2.30 Purchase, Jan. 15 352 $2.50 Purchase, Feb. 4 101 $2.20 Purchase, Feb. 14 645 $2.00 Find the value of widgets in their inventory using the FIFO method of inventory.

options: Statement 1 is True, Statement 2 is False

attempted_job.description: A young man suggested to his friend that they steal a large-screen TV from a neighborÕs house. The friend was angry with the young man and decided to use the opportunity to get even with him by having him arrested. The friend said he would help, and that night, he drove the young man to the neighborÕs house. The young man broke in while the friend remained out-side. The friend called the police on his cell phone and then drove away. Police officers arrived at the scene just as the young man was carrying the TV out the back door. The jurisdiction defines crimes as at common law. Of what crime, if any, can the friend properly be convicted?

The reaction of Br2(g) with Cl2(g) to form BrCl(g) has an equilibrium constant of 15.0 at a certain temperature. If 10.0 grams of BrCl is initially present in a 15.0-liter reaction vessel, what will the concentration of BrCl be at equilibrium?

Alex needs to borrow $\$10,\!000$ from the bank. The bank gives him two options. 1. A ten-year loan with an annual interest rate of $10\%$ compounded quarterly, with the condition that at the end of 5 years, Alex must make a payment equal to half of what he owes. The other half continues to accrue interest, and at the end of the ten years, Alex will pay off the remaining balance. 2. A ten-year loan with a simple annual interest rate of $12\%$, with just one lump-sum payment at the end of the ten years. Find the positive difference between the total amounts Alex has to pay back under the two schemes. Round your answer to the nearest dollar.

The reaction of Br2(g) with Cl2(g) to form BrCl(g) has an equilibrium constant of 15.0 at a certain temperature. If 10.0 grams of BrCl is initially present in a 15.0-liter reaction vessel, what will the concentration of BrCl be at equilibrium?

Air at one atm. and 90°F moves at 140 ft/sec across a flat plate of length 3 ft. The temperature at the sur-face of the plate is 450°F. For a 1 ft. width, deter-mine the heat loss from the entire plate, and also the heat loss from the laminar and turbulent sections of the boundary layer. Then, assuming that the boundary layer is completely turbulent, calculate the heat loss.
Which of the following is not among the methods unions use to increase wages?
options: Seeking to increase the demand for the union's labor
Negotiations to obtain a wage floor
Encouraging workers to increase their productivity
Lobbying for higher minimum wage laws
Efforts to decrease the prices of substitute resources
attempted_job.correct_answer: Efforts to decrease the prices of substitute resources

This question refers to the following information.
Read the documents below.
Source 1
If then by the Use of Machines, the Manufacture of Cotton, an Article which we import, and are supplied with from other Countries, and which can everywhere be procured on equal Terms, has met with such amazing Success, may not greater Advantages be reasonably expected from cultivating to the utmost the Manufacture of Wool, the Produce of our own Island, an Article in Demand in all Countries, almost the universal Clothing of Mankind?
In the Manufacture of Woollens, the Scribbling Mill, the Spinning Frame, and the Fly Shuttle, have reduced manual Labour nearly One third, and each of them at its-first Introduction carried an Alarm to the Work People, yet each has contributed to advance the Wages and to increase the Trade, so that if an Attempt was now made to deprive us of the Use of them, there is no Doubt, but every Person engaged in the Business, would exert himself to defend them.
—Statement by the Cloth Merchants of Leeds, 1791
Source 2
Come, cropper lads of high renown,
Who love to drink good ale that's brown,
And strike each haughty tyrant down,
With hatchet, pike, and gun!
Oh, the cropper lads for me,
The gallant lads for me,
Who with lusty stroke,
The shear frames broke,
The cropper lads for me!
What though the specials still advance,
And soldiers nightly round us prance;
The cropper lads still lead the dance,
With hatchet, pike, and gun!
Oh, the cropper lads for me,
The gallant lads for me,
Who with lusty stroke
The shear frames broke,
The cropper lads for me!
—Luddite Song, The Cropper's Song, c. 1812
Which of the following economic theories is Source 1 above referencing in support of the expansion and use of machines?
What do we know about the people who built Stonehenge based on the analysis of animal teeth from a nearby village?
options: They had no domesticated animals but hunted wild animals for meat.
They had domesticated animals, but they did not consume their meat.
They bred and raised a specific species of deer for food.
They were vegetarian and did not consume meat.
They brought animals to the site from outside of the region.
They raised livestock at the site, including cattle, sheep, and goats.
They hunted antelope, gazelle, and bear.
They imported exotic animals from distant lands.
They primarily raised and hunted aquatic animals.
They hunted only large game animals, such as mammoths and bison.
attempted_job.correct_answer: They brought animals to the site from outside of the region.

The screwhas a mean; diameter of 18 mm. and has a single trapezoidalmetric thread cut with a pitch of 4 mm. Determine thetorque required to raise a load of 500 kg, if both the collarand the threads have a coefficient of friction of 0.3.

This question refers to the following information.
Oh, shame on you, poor Winter King!
What's this that you have done?
Is 't not a very naughty thing
To snatch the kaiser's crown?
Now you will have to stay away
Alike from Rhine and Prague,
And more than that-shame and dismay
Your days and nights will plague.
Right well you knew, and all the world,
Right well they know this thing,
That Ferdinand alone can be
Bohemia's lawful king.
So come, dear Fritz, rouse up and go
To Ferdinand, your king,
And beg him graciously to show
Full pardon for your sin.
"Poor Winter King," seventeenth-century song
Which of the following is an important underlying issue in this song?

Suppose there are three routers between a source host and a destination host. Ignoring fragmentation, an IP datagram sent from the source host to the destination host will travel over how many interfaces? How many forwarding tables will be indexed to move the datagram from the source to the destination? Answer in [Interfaces, Tables].

Statement 1 is a theorem in real analysis, Statement 2 is a principle in information theory

attempted_job.description: Find the fraction of 7.7-MeV alpha particles that is deflected at an angle of 90° or more from a gold foil of 10^-6 m thickness.

A defendant met her friend at the electronics store where the friend worked. Unbeknownst to the defendant, her friend had taken a music player from the display case and stuffed it in her bag earlier in the day. Knowing that employees are subject to search when they leave the store, the friend surreptitiously put the music player into the defendant's bag. The defendant and her friend proceeded to a local tavern and drank shots of tequila. The friend became intoxicated and forgot to retrieve the stolen music player from the defendant's bag. After leaving the tavern, the defendant proceeded home. Upon opening her bag, she was surprised to see the music player. Believing that the music player had accidentally fallen into her bag at the bar, the defendant planned to return it the next day. The following day, however, the defendant changed her mind and decided to keep it. The defendant is guilty of
options: neither receiving stolen property nor larceny.
attempted_job.correct_answer: neither receiving stolen property nor larceny.

attempted_job.description: This biome has cold winters and is known for its pine forests.

Frank Lee and Steve Barnes are partners. Barnes receives a weekly salary of $300, and 1/3 of the remaining net income. The other 2/3 of the remaining income goes to Lee. If the profits are $28,269, how much does each partner receive?

On his 21st birthday, a son enlisted in the army. As he was about to leave for eight weeks of basic training, his father said, "I will buy you a new car at the end of basic training if you promise me not to take illegal drugs or drink alcohol while at basic training. " The son replied, "You can order the new car right away. I promise not to take illegal drugs or drink alcohol, as you requested. "At the end of basic training, the son was given a leave and returned home. His father asked, "Did you abide by your promise not to take illegal drugs or drink alcohol?" The son replied, "Yes, Father. " The father then told the son that he had already ordered the new car and that it would be available for delivery within one month. One week later, the son received the tragic news that his father had died suddenly. At the funeral, the executor of the father's estate told the son that he did not feel compelled to give the son the newly arrived car. In a suit against the executor of the father's estate to recover the new car, the son will
options: not succeed, because the father's death nullifies his promise.
not succeed, because the father's promise was void.
not succeed, because the executor of the estate has the final say.
not succeed, because the son's promise did not constitute a legal contract.
succeed, because the son's promise to refrain from drinking alcohol provided sufficient consideration.
not succeed, because the father's promise was only a conditional gift.
attempted_job.correct_answer: succeed, because the son's promise to refrain from drinking alcohol provided sufficient consideration.

attempted_job.description: A high school junior was charged by the school administration with violating certain sections of the disciplinary code, specifically, he was charged with being disrespectful to a teacher by using profanity and with using abusive language to a fellow student. The principal, sent the student's parents a letter notifying them of the three-day suspension for the above-mentioned charges. The suspension was to take effect on February 1. The principal also included a copy of the disciplinary code in the letter. On January 19, the student and his mother met with the principal in his office to discuss the matter, and the student admitted that he used abusive language to a fellow student. On January 22, the student's parents received a letter informing them that his teacher had upheld the school administration's decision to suspend their son. They were then notified of a hearing on the recommended suspension to be held at the school. The parents did not attend this hearing, but were advised that the school board upheld the suspension, effective February 1. Which of the following most accurately summarizes the applicable rule of constitutional law with respect to the student's suspension?
options: The student's suspension did not constitute a denial of due process.
The student's suspension was a violation of his Fifth Amendment rights to due process.
The school did not have the right to suspend the student without the presence of his parents at the hearing.
The student's suspension was a violation of the Eighth Amendment's prohibition of cruel and unusual punishment.
The student's suspension violated his right to a fair trial.
The disciplinary code violated the student's Fourth Amendment rights against unreasonable searches and seizures.
The disciplinary code violated the student's right to a compulsory school education.
The school administration's decision was protected under the Tenth Amendment's guarantee of states' rights.
The student's suspension deprived him of liberty and property without due process, as guaranteed by the Fourteenth Amendment.
The student's conduct was protected under the First Amendment's guarantee of freedom of speech.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The student's suspension did not constitute a denial of due process.

attempted_job.description: Two spaceships approach Earth with equal speeds, as measured by an observer on Earth, but from opposite directions. A meterstick on one spaceship is measured to be 60 cm long by an occupant of the other spaceship. What is the speed of each spaceship, as measured by the observer on Earth?
How do viruses of the calcivirus family replicate?
options: By integrating their DNA into the host cell's genome

Given a population in genetic equilibrium in which the initial gene frequency of d is 0.2; assume the rate of mutation (u) of D \rightarrow d to be 4.1 × 10^-5, and the rate of back mutation (v) of d \rightarrow D to be 2.5 × 10^-7. (a) If the above rates of mutation are introduced into the population, what will be the change in q (frequency of d) in the first generation? (b) Assuming that the above rates continue over time, what will be the value of q at mutational equilibrium?
Identify the antecedent of the following conditional proposition: The Bees win their first game only if either the Aardvarks or the Chipmunks do not win their first games.
options: The Bees do not win their first game.
Both the Aardvarks and the Chipmunks do not win their first games.
The Chipmunks do not win their first game.
The Chipmunks win their first game.
Both the Aardvarks and the Chipmunks win their first games.
Either the Aardvarks or the Chipmunks do not win their first games.
The Aardvarks do not win their first game.
The Bees win their first game.
The Aardvarks win their first game.
Either the Aardvarks or the Chipmunks win their first games.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The Bees win their first game.

In client-centered case consultation, the consultant's primary goal is to:
options: work independently to provide the optimal intervention to a client.
help the consultee develop a stronger professional identity and sense of self.
help the consultee identify methods for evaluating his/her own professional services.
help the consultee to improve his/her communication skills with clients.
help the consultee determine how to work more effectively with a particular client.
help the consultee learn to manage his/her own professional stress and burnout.
help the consultee establish a structured plan for future client interactions.
work collaboratively with the consultee in delivering the optimal intervention to a client.
help a consultee enhance his/her skills and knowledge so that the consultee functions more effectively in the future.
help the consultee identify areas of personal growth and development.
attempted_job.correct_answer: help the consultee determine how to work more effectively with a particular client.

attempted_job.description: The product of two of the four roots of the quartic equation x^4 - 18x^3 +kx2 + 200x - 1984 = 0 is -32. Determine the value of k.

Dr. Delbert Dinwoody is currently treating a 35-year-old client who has received a diagnosis of Alcohol Dependence. With the client's permission, Dr. Dinwoody invites the client's wife to participate in couple therapy. Inviting the wife to participate in therapy is:
options: acceptable as long as the client and wife are not separated or in the process of divorce.
acceptable as long as the wife is not receiving mental health services from another professional.
"unacceptable because in this situation, the wife is “vulnerable to undue inﬂuence."""
acceptable only if the wife has also been diagnosed with Alcohol Dependence.
acceptable as long as he believes couple therapy will benefit the client's progress in dealing with his alcohol problem.
unacceptable because the wife should seek her own professional help.
"unacceptable because doing so creates a ""conﬂict of interest.”"
acceptable only if the wife gives her written consent to participate in the therapy.
unacceptable because it may create a bias in the therapist's approach towards the client.
unacceptable because it infringes on the client's individual therapy process.
attempted_job.correct_answer: acceptable as long as he believes couple therapy will benefit the client's progress in dealing with his alcohol problem.

attempted_job.description: Which of the following statements about floating-point arithmetic is NOT true?
options: It is inherently nonassociative because some numbers have no exact representation.
All operations in floating-point arithmetic are commutative.
It is inherently nonassociative because there have to be upper and lower bounds on the size of numbers.
Associativity can be achieved with appropriate roundoff conventions.
Some rational numbers have no exact representation.
attempted_job.correct_answer: Associativity can be achieved with appropriate roundoff conventions.

options: False, False, but there are exceptions

attempted_job.description: John Dalton found water to be 12.5% hydrogen by weight. Calculate the atomic weight of oxygen if Dalton assumed water contained two hydrogen atoms for every three oxygen atoms. Assume 1 H atom weighs 1amu.

This question refers to the following information.
Read the following governmental report.
Of the 450 sick persons whom the inhabitants were unable to relieve, 200 were turned out, and these we saw die one by one as they lay on the roadside. A large number still remain, and to each of them it is only possible to dole out the least scrap of bread. We only give bread to those who would otherwise die. The staple dish here consists of mice, which the inhabitants hunt, so desperate are they from hunger. They devour roots which the animals cannot eat; one can, in fact, not put into words the things one sees. . . . This narrative, far from exaggerating, rather understates the horror of the case, for it does not record the hundredth part of the misery in this district. Those who have not witnessed it with their own eyes cannot imagine how great it is. Not a day passes but at least 200 people die of famine in the two provinces. We certify to having ourselves seen herds, not of cattle, but of men and women, wandering about the fields between Rheims and Rhétel, turning up the earth like pigs to find a few roots; and as they can only find rotten ones, and not half enough of them, they become so weak that they have not strength left to seek food. The parish priest at Boult, whose letter we enclose, tells us he has buried three of his parishioners who died of hunger. The rest subsisted on chopped straw mixed with earth, of which they composed a food which cannot be called bread. Other persons in the same place lived on the bodies of animals which had died of disease, and which the curé, otherwise unable to help his people, allowed them to roast at the presbytery fire.
—Report of the Estates of Normandy, 1651
Which of the following would be most responsible for ending the problems of hunger mentioned above?
options: The development of a welfare system to support those in need
The increase in population during the seventeenth century leading to more workers in the field
The discovery of new trade routes that brought in more food
The elimination of fallow fields through the agricultural revolution during the seventeenth century
The introduction of new crops from the New World during the Columbian Exchange
The implementation of a universal basic income
The elimination of the Black Plague in Europe during the seventeenth century
The creation of a strong putting-out system that allowed people to be paid for their work
A declining population during the seventeenth century that led to better food yields and less hunger as Europe urbanized
attempted_job.correct_answer: The elimination of fallow fields through the agricultural revolution during the seventeenth century

attempted_job.description: The fact that early excavators of Teotihuacán mistook ordinary housing compounds for palaces suggests:
options: the society was a theocracy, ruled by high priests.
commoners took over the residences of the elite.
the city was a democracy, ruled by common farmers and artisans.
the general standard of living was high.
the elite lived in ordinary housing compounds to blend in with the commoners.
the housing compounds were built with materials typically used for palaces.
the city's economic system was based on wealth distribution.
the city was abandoned and later occupied by a different civilization.
the society was mostly egalitarian, with almost no social stratification.
the excavators were inexperienced in identifying historical structures.
attempted_job.correct_answer: the general standard of living was high.

attempted_job.description: Ethane reacts with astoichiometricamount of air by the followingcombustion equation : C_2H_6 + 3.5O_2 + 3.5(3.76)N_2\rightharpoonup 2CO_2 + 3H_2O + 3.5(3.76)N_2. Using the following enthalpy data, calculate the amount of heattransfer per mole of fuel if ethane enters at 60 C, the air entersat 4.4°C and the products leave at 449°C.
Which of the following is a binomial random variable?
options: The number of motorists not wearing seat belts in a random sample of five drivers.
The number of hearts out of five cards randomly drawn from a deck of 52 cards, without replacement.
The number of tosses before a "5" appears when tossing a fair die.
The number of green marbles drawn from a bag containing red, blue, and green marbles.
The number of points a hockey team receives in 10 games, where two points are awarded for wins, one point for ties, and no points for losses.
The number of red cards in a random hand of five cards drawn from a deck of 52 cards.
The time it takes to complete a 1000-piece puzzle.
The number of cars of a specific model sold in a month at a car dealership.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The number of motorists not wearing seat belts in a random sample of five drivers.

attempted_job.description: How long would it take a car, starting from rest and accelerating uniformly in a straight line at 5 m/s^2, to cover a distance of 200 m ?

The $J=2$ to 3 rotational transition in a certain diatomic molecule occurs at $126.4 \mathrm{GHz}$, where $1 \mathrm{GHz} \equiv 10^9 \mathrm{~Hz}$. Find the frequency of the $J=5$ to 6 absorption in this molecule.
The proportion of babies that have an abnormality identifiable at birth is _____.

A woman owned land in fee simple absolute. The woman conveyed the land to a friend "for life," and when the friend died the land was to go to the woman's neighbor "and her heirs." The neighbor died and in her duly probated will devised her entire estate to a local charity. If she had died intestate, her daughter would have been her only heir. One year after the neighbor died, her daughter executed a quitclaim deed conveying any interest she might have in the land to the woman's friend. The common law Rule Against Perpetuities is unmodified in the jurisdiction. There are no other applicable statutes. Who has what interest in the land?
options: The friend has a life estate and the woman has a reversion, because the neighbor's remainder was void under the Rule Against Perpetuities.
The friend has a fee simple absolute, because his life estate merged with the remainder conveyed to him by the daughter.
The friend has a life estate and the charity has a contingent remainder, because the neighbor's interest was devisable and her will transferred her entire estate.
The friend has a fee simple absolute, because the quitclaim deed from the daughter transferred any interest she might have had in the land.
The friend has a life estate and the daughter has a contingent remainder, because the deed from the woman created an interest in the neighbor's heirs.
The friend has a life estate and the daughter has a vested remainder, because the deed from the woman created an interest in the neighbor's heirs.
The friend has a life estate and the woman's neighbor has a vested remainder, because the deed from the woman created an interest in the neighbor.
The friend has a life estate and the charity has a vested remainder, because the neighbor's interest was devisable.
The friend has a life estate and the local charity has a contingent remainder, because the neighbor's will transferred her entire estate.
The friend has a life estate and the woman's neighbor has a contingent remainder, because the neighbor's will devised her entire estate to a local charity.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The friend has a life estate and the charity has a vested remainder, because the neighbor's interest was devisable.

attempted_job.description: In a study of the equilibrium, H_2 + I_2 = 2HI, a certain number of moles x of HI are formed when 1 mole of H_2 and 3 moles of I_2 are introduced into a flask of volume V at temperature T. On introducing 2 additional moles of H_2, the amount of HI formed is found to be 2x. CalculateK_p.
When the price of pears increases, we expect the following:
options: There is no change in the quantity demanded or supplied.
There is a surplus of pears in the market.

attempted_job.description:
Construct a complete truth table for the following argument. Then, using the truth table, determine whether the argument is valid or invalid. If the argument is invalid, choose an option which presents a counterexample. (There may be other counterexamples as well.)
~A · ~B
(A ∨ C) ∨ B / C
options: Invalid. Counterexample when A, B, and C are all true
Invalid. Counterexample when A, B, and C are all false
Invalid. Counterexample when A and B are true and C is false
Invalid. Counterexample when B is true and A and C are false
Invalid. Counterexample when A and C are true and B is false
Invalid. Counterexample when A is true and B and C are false
Invalid. Counterexample when A is false and B and C are true
Invalid. Counterexample when C is true and A and B are false

Assume that all gases are perfect and that data refer to 298 K unless otherwise stated. 3.17 Estimate the standard reaction Gibbs energy of $\mathrm{N}_2(\mathrm{~g})+3 \mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightarrow$ $2 \mathrm{NH}_3(\mathrm{~g})$ at $1000 \mathrm{~K}$ from their values at $298 \mathrm{~K}$.
Name and describe three theories of intelligence.
options: Spatial intelligence theory, Abstract intelligence theory, Musical intelligence theory
Analytic intelligence theory, Creative intelligence theory, Contextual intelligence theory
Fluid intelligence theory, Crystallized intelligence theory, Reflective intelligence theory
Two-factor theory of intelligence, Multiple-factor theory, Hierarchical theory of intelligence
Emotional intelligence theory, Social intelligence theory, Fluid and crystallized intelligence
Biological theory of intelligence, Psychometric theory of intelligence, Distributed intelligence theory
General intelligence theory, Emotional intelligence theory, Kinesthetic intelligence theory
Interpersonal intelligence theory, Intrapersonal intelligence theory, Experiential intelligence theory
Theory of multiple intelligences, Triarchic theory of intelligence, PASS theory
Cognitive intelligence theory, Linguistic intelligence theory, Practical intelligence theory
attempted_job.correct_answer: Two-factor theory of intelligence, Multiple-factor theory, Hierarchical theory of intelligence

attempted_job.description: What are the symptoms of a paranoiddisorder ?Discuss the threetypes of this disorder.
options: The symptoms of paranoid disorder are persistent, persecutory delusions or delusions of jealousy. The three types of paranoid disorders are paranoia, shared paranoia, and paranoid state.
Symptoms include hallucinations and mood swings. The three types are delusional disorder, shared delusion, and paranoia
Symptoms are delusions and hallucinations. Types include paranoid schizophrenia, paranoid personality disorder, and delusional disorder.
Symptoms are distrust and communication difficulties. The types include paranoid anxiety, paranoid personality disorder, and paranoid psychosis.
The symptoms include obsessive thoughts and compulsive behaviors. The types are shared paranoia, paranoid personality disorder, and paranoia
Symptoms are anxiety and depression. Types include shared paranoia, paranoia, and paranoid personality disorder
The symptoms include chronic anxiety and mistrust. The types are paranoid schizophrenia, paranoia, and paranoid psychosis.
The symptoms include extreme suspicion and social isolation. The types are delusional disorder, paranoia, and paranoid anxiety disorder.
Symptoms include persistent fear and social withdrawal. The types are paranoid psychosis, shared paranoid disorder, and paranoid depression.
The symptoms of paranoid disorder are delusions of grandeur and auditory hallucinations. The three types are paranoid personality disorder, paranoid schizophrenia, and paranoia.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The symptoms of paranoid disorder are persistent, persecutory delusions or delusions of jealousy. The three types of paranoid disorders are paranoia, shared paranoia, and paranoid state.

attempted_job.description: Electromagnetic radiation provides a means to probe aspects of the physical universe. Which of the following statements regarding radiation spectra is NOT correct?
options: All spectra are essentially the same for all elements.
The wavelengths identified in an absorption spectrum of an element are among those in its emission spectrum.
Spectral analysis can be used to identify the composition of galactic dust.
Spectral lines in the radio frequency region reveal the rotational structure of molecules.
Absorption and emission spectra of an element are always identical.
Lines in the infrared, visible, and ultraviolet regions of the spectrum reveal primarily the nuclear structure of the sample.
Emission spectra can be used to determine the temperature of a star.
X-ray spectroscopy is used primarily to identify the presence of biological molecules.
Spectral analysis can't determine the relative abundance of elements in a sample.
Absorption spectra can be used to determine which elements are present in distant stars.
attempted_job.correct_answer: Lines in the infrared, visible, and ultraviolet regions of the spectrum reveal primarily the nuclear structure of the sample.

attempted_job.description:
This question refers to the following information.
Literacy rates among the Russian-speaking population of late Imperial Russia and the Soviet Union, 1897–1955
Literacy Rate
1897  24%
1917  45%
1926  56%
1937  75%
1939  81.10%
1955  99.90%
Source: Census data and Soviet Ministry of Education
Which of the following segments of the Russian/Soviet population would probably have benefited most from the rising literacy rates depicted in the chart?
options: Members of the Russian Orthodox clergy
Members of the Russian Imperial family
Artists and writers of the time

attempted_job.description: A man was under custodial interrogation as a suspect in the murder of a child. The police initially gave him a written list of his Miranda rights. They told him to read the list out loud, which he did. They then asked him if he understood the right to remain silent and to have a lawyer present and he nodded affirmatively. He refused to sign the writing but did not ask for an attorney. During the next three hours they interrogated him and got few answers; he was largely silent but did respond at times with a "no" or "yes" or a nod of the head. Then one of the detectives asked him if he was ready to ask God for forgiveness for killing the child. His eyes welled with tears and he said "yes." This opened further questioning leading to his admission of guilt. He would not sign a written confession or a waiver of the right to remain silent. He was arrested for murder and moved to suppress the confession. Will the court suppress the confession as being involuntarily given?
options: No, the suspect's emotional response and admission of guilt indicates a voluntary confession.
Yes, the confession is considered involuntary because he did not sign a written waiver of his right to remain silent.
Yes, the suspect's refusal to sign the written confession and waiver of rights indicates the confession was involuntary.
No, the suspect's engagement in the questioning, although minimal, demonstrates an understanding and voluntary waiver of his rights.
No, the confession is not considered involuntary as the suspect voluntarily answered the questions without the presence of an attorney.
Yes, the confession must be suppressed because the suspect was largely silent during the interrogation, indicating a lack of willingness to participate.
Yes, the use of religious deception to get a suspect to admit to crime renders the confession involuntary.
No, the circumstances indicate that he understood that he could stop the questioning and get a lawyer but he did not do so.
Yes, the confession must be suppressed because he was questioned for over three hours, making anything he said essentially involuntary.
No, whenever a written list of Miranda warnings is given to a suspect and he doesn't ask for a lawyer, this is proof that the suspect fully understands his rights.
attempted_job.correct_answer: No, the circumstances indicate that he understood that he could stop the questioning and get a lawyer but he did not do so.

Assuming that gene frequencies for this population are found to be: p (frequency of F) = 0.7 and q (frequency of f) = 0.3, and that the population is in genetic equilibrium, what proportion of the progeny produced frommatingsof full-podded× full-poddedwill be constricted-podded?

(T · ~U) ⊃ ~W
~W ⊃ X
~Z ⊃ W
~(X · Z) / T ⊃ U
options: Invalid. Counterexample when T and X are true and U, W, and Z are false
Invalid. Counterexample when W, X, and Z are true and T and U are false
Invalid. Counterexample when T, U, and X are true and W and Z are false
Invalid. Counterexample when T, U, and W are true and X and Z are false
Invalid. Counterexample when T, W, and Z are true and U and X are false
Invalid. Counterexample when T, U, and Z are true and W and X are false
Invalid. Counterexample when T, W, and X are true and U and Z are false

attempted_job.description: Baron cites a summary of Ali Soufan's Senate testimony to suggest that
options: torture is always wrong without exception.
torture is the most effective method of interrogation.
successful interrogation may not require torture.
all successful interrogations involve some form of torture.
Ali Soufan was subjected to torture during his testimony.
Ali Soufan denounced the use of torture entirely in his testimony.
endorsing a single act of torture requires endorsing torture as a practice.
Ali Soufan supports the use of torture in interrogation.
torture can be ethically justified in some situations.
torture works in only limited situations.
attempted_job.correct_answer: successful interrogation may not require torture.

attempted_job.description: How is incoming sensory verbal information abstracted in terms of classification?
options: The nervous system classifies information based on emotional response
The nervous system classifies information over a period of time
The nervous system classifies information randomly
The nervous system classifies information based on the type of senses
The nervous system classifies information based on previous experiences only
The nervous system classifies information instantly
The nervous system classifies information using a single, universal category
The nervous system classifies information according to the body part receiving the stimulus
The nervous system does not classify information
The nervous system classifies information based on the intensity of the stimulus
attempted_job.correct_answer: The nervous system classifies information over a period of time

attempted_job.description: A method is to be written to search an array for a value that is larger than a given item and return its index. The problem specification does not indicate what should be returned if there are several such values in the array. Which of the following actions would be best?
options: The method should return an error if more than one larger value is found.
The specification should be modified to indicate what should be done if there is more than one index of larger values.
The method should be written to output a message if more than one larger value is found.
The method should be written so as to return the index of every occurrence of a larger value.
The method should be written to return the last occurrence of a larger value.
The method should return a random index of a larger value.
The method should be written to return the largest index among larger values.
The method should be written on the assumption that there is only one value in the array that is larger than the given item.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The specification should be modified to indicate what should be done if there is more than one index of larger values.

attempted_job.description: Nancy has the choice to spend one hour studying for an exam, mowing the lawn for one hour at a wage of $6, or babysitting her niece for one hour at a wage of $8. If we know that Nancy has chosen to study for the exam, which of the following is true?
options: The opportunity cost of studying is $6, which is equal to the benefit received from studying.
The opportunity cost of studying is $14, which is less than the benefit received from studying.
Nancy has no other options available besides studying, mowing the lawn, or babysitting.
Nancy values studying more than earning $6 from mowing the lawn.
Nancy is indifferent to both studying and mowing the lawn.
Nancy's behavior is irrational, since babysitting was clearly superior to all other options.
The benefit received from studying is less than the opportunity cost of $8.
The benefit received from studying is greater than the opportunity cost of $8.
The opportunity cost of studying is $8, but the benefit of studying is unmeasurable.
Nancy prefers babysitting to studying but chose to study instead.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The benefit received from studying is greater than the opportunity cost of $8.

attempted_job.description: A young man suggested to his friend that they steal a large-screen TV from a neighborÕs house. The friend was angry with the young man and decided to use the opportunity to get even with him by having him arrested. The friend said he would help, and that night, he drove the young man to the neighborÕs house. The young man broke in while the friend remained out-side. The friend called the police on his cell phone and then drove away. Police officers arrived at the scene just as the young man was carrying the TV out the back door. The jurisdiction defines crimes as at common law. Of what crime, if any, can the friend properly be convicted?

Suppose the marginal utility to David Jones of product A is defined by the function, MU_A = 10 - x, where x is the number of units of A pro-duced. For B, MU_B = 21 - 2y, where y is the number of units of B produced. Assume that the price of A = price of B = $1. How much of A and B would David buy if he had $7 to spend?
options: Two units of product A and four units of product B
one unit of product A and six units of product B
two units of product A and five units of product B
Three units of product A and three units of product B
Zero units of product A and seven units of product B
Five units of product A and two units of product B
three units of product A and four units of product B
Six units of product A and one unit of product B
four units of product A and three units of product B
Seven units of product A and zero units of product B
attempted_job.correct_answer: one unit of product A and six units of product B

The corona is made up mostly of charged particles which aren't luminous.
The Sun's corona is actually invisible to the human eye

attempted_job.description: What is the inflation tax ?
options: The inflation tax is an annual fee paid by individuals directly to the government to compensate for inflation.
The inflation tax is a tax imposed by the government to control inflation
The inflation tax is the hypothetical revenue the government earns from issuing more money.
The inflation tax refers to the increased cost of goods and services that consumers face during periods of high inflation.
The inflation tax is a tax levied on businesses during periods of high inflation
The inflation tax is the reduced purchasing power of money held by the public due to an increase in the overall price level.
The value of the inflation tax is the amount that individuals have to add to their cash balances every year to keep the real value of their cash balances constant.
The inflation tax is the amount that individuals have to deduct from their cash balances every year due to inflation
The inflation tax is a specific percentage of income tax rates adjusted according to the inflation rate.
The inflation tax is a one-time surcharge on savings to reduce the money supply and combat inflation.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The value of the inflation tax is the amount that individuals have to add to their cash balances every year to keep the real value of their cash balances constant.

The Shang Dynasty laid the foundation for a coherent development of Chinese civilization that lasted well into the:
Bronchial breathing is characterized by
options: Inspiratory component louder and longer with a gap between inspiration and expiration
Inspiratory component louder and longer, but no gap between expiration and inspiration
Expiratory component louder and longer, but no gap between expiration and inspiration
Both inspiratory and expiratory components are equal in length and volume
Inspiratory component louder and longer with a gap between expiration and inspiration
Both inspiratory and expiratory components are equal with no gap between them
Expiratory component louder and longer with a gap between inspiration and expiration
Expiratory component louder and longer with a gap between expiration and inspiration
Inspiratory component shorter and quieter with a gap between expiration and inspiration
Expiratory component shorter and quieter with a gap between inspiration and expiration
attempted_job.correct_answer: Expiratory component louder and longer with a gap between inspiration and expiration

attempted_job.description: Papadaki argues that
options: sexual objectification is not always wrong.
sexual objectification involves the total reduction of a person to an object.
sexual objectification is not explainable in Kantian terms.
sexual objectification involves the denial of a person's humanity.
attempted_job.correct_answer: sexual objectification involves the denial of a person's humanity.

attempted_job.description: Which single statement about fat digestion and absorption is correct?
options: Absorption of fatty acids in the intestine occurs mainly through FABP and FAT/CD36 transport proteins in the enterocytes of the distal duodenum
After absorption, medium chain triglycerides (MCT) enter the lymphatic system, whereas long chain triglycerides enter the portal blood system.
The products of partial digestion of triglycerides (triacylglycerols) can form mixed micelles with pancreatic cholesterol and phospholipids and this increases absorption
Triglycerides (triacylglycerols) must be emulsified and digested before the fatty acids can be transported
attempted_job.correct_answer: Triglycerides (triacylglycerols) must be emulsified and digested before the fatty acids can be transported

attempted_job.description:
This question refers to the following information.
"The only force which is strong enough to break down social convention is economic necessity. . . . The economic necessity which has forced women out of the home and into the world of business has completely annihilated the old idea that a woman should eat only in the privacy of her household or in the homes of her friends, has created the absolutely new social phenomenon of women eating in public, unescorted by men, by the tens of thousands, and has given rise to a wholly new phase of the restaurant business."
—The New York Times, October 15, 1905

In Japan, the frequency of the R-governed multiple drug-resistant strain ofShigella, which causes dysentery, rose from 0.2 percent in 1953 to 58 percent in 1965. Also, 84 percent of the E. coli and 90 percent of Proteus collected from affected hospital patients showed similar resistance. How could such an increase happen?
options: The bacteria developed immunity to antibiotics
Transfer of resistance genes from non-pathogenic to pathogenic bacteria through horizontal gene transfer
A decline in vaccination rates leading to increased bacterial spread
Over-prescription of antibiotics in livestock agriculture leading to cross-species transfer of resistance
Natural selection favored bacteria that were inherently resistant to antibiotics
Increased use of antibacterial soaps and disinfectants in hospitals
Introduction of new antibiotics that were less effective against resistant strains
Indiscriminate use of antibiotics selected for drug-resistant bacteria which can then pass their resistance to potentially harmful strains.
attempted_job.correct_answer: Indiscriminate use of antibiotics selected for drug-resistant bacteria which can then pass their resistance to potentially harmful strains.

An examinee who makes a number of confabulatory responses on the Rorschach has overgeneralized from a part of the inkblot to the whole. Such responses suggest which of the following?
options: an inability to perceive abstract concepts
high level of stress or anxiety
a lack of attention to detail
brain damage, intellectual disability, or emotional disturbance
a high degree of defensiveness or denial
a high degree of introversion or social withdrawal
attempted_job.correct_answer: brain damage, intellectual disability, or emotional disturbance

attempted_job.description: A man and a woman who were friends decided to buy a car together. The man put $5,000 down and the woman's father co-signed on the loan of $10,000. The title was put in the woman's name and the man made the loan payments. They orally agreed that it was a shared ownership, with equal rights. After several months, their relationship deteriorated. They argued over ownership and use of the car. One day, the man attempted to leave with the car, but the woman climbed in and kept arguing as he was driving away. He then crashed into a pole and destroyed the car just to spite her, saying that if he couldn't have it no one would have it. The authorities arrested the man for auto larceny (stealing a car from the owner) and criminal mischief (damaging property of another or property shared with another). Assuming the accuracy of the foregoing facts beyond a reasonable doubt, which crime or crimes alleged can likely result in a sustainable conviction?
options: He can be convicted of a different crime not listed.
He can be convicted of criminal mischief and a different crime not listed.
He cannot be convicted of larceny, but he can be convicted of a different crime not listed.
He cannot be convicted of any crime.
He can be convicted of both larceny and criminal mischief.
He can be convicted of larceny but he cannot be convicted of criminal mischief.
He can be convicted of both larceny and a different crime not listed.
He can be convicted of criminal mischief only.
He can be convicted of larceny only.
He can be convicted of neither larceny nor criminal mischief due to shared ownership.
attempted_job.correct_answer: He can be convicted of criminal mischief only.

Find the distance between a plane passing through the centers of one close-packed layer of spheres and an-other plane passing through the centers of an adjacent close- packed layer. You may assume that all spheres are identical with a radius of .200 nm.
Which of the following statements is not correct about lipids?
options: Lipids consist of fatty acids and glycerol.
The molecules of a saturated fatty acid are packed so close together that they form a solid at room temperature.
The head of a phospholipid is hydrophobic, and the tails are hydrophilic.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The head of a phospholipid is hydrophobic, and the tails are hydrophilic.
